title: phase 1: backend foundation — fastapi app, sqlalchemy, orm models, pydantic schemas, alembic migrations, health endpoints
description: Stand up the full backend skeleton as described in spec.md phase 1. Includes: monorepo structure (backend/, shared/, docker-compose.yml, Dockerfiles, .env.example), FastAPI app entry point (backend/app/main.py), core modules (core/database.py, core/config.py, core/events.py), all SQLAlchemy ORM models (project, config_version, model_profile, dataset_profile, run, run_stage, metric_point, artifact, ai_suggestion, decision_log, activation_snapshot, storage_record), all Pydantic schemas in backend/app/schemas/, Alembic migration setup with initial migration, health endpoints (GET /health, GET /health/system), pyproject.toml with all dependencies from spec.md section 8.3. Must pass ruff linting. No frontend work.

title: phase 1: frontend foundation — react+vite+ts+shadcn setup, routing, layout shell, zustand stores, tailwind
description: Stand up the full frontend skeleton as described in spec.md phase 1. Includes: Vite+React+TypeScript project in frontend/ with all dependencies from spec.md section 8.2 (shadcn/ui, tailwind 3, recharts, tanstack-query 5, zustand 4, react-router-dom 6, cmdk, react-resizable-panels, @monaco-editor/react, lucide-react), App.tsx with routing for all 12 screens (Dashboard, Projects, Models, Datasets, Training, Adapters, Weights, Runs, Compare, AI Suggestions, Artifacts, Settings), layout shell with left sidebar nav (collapsible to icons), central workspace, right drawer (toggleable), bottom panel (resizable, collapsible), Zustand stores for client-side UI state (active project, sidebar state, drawer state, panel visibility), all TypeScript type definition files in frontend/src/types/ (one per domain: project, config, run, model, dataset, artifact, suggestion, websocket), placeholder page components for all 12 screens, Dockerfile.frontend. Must pass prettier and TypeScript type check. No backend integration needed — use placeholder/mock shapes.

title: phase 2: project crud — backend rest endpoints + pydantic schemas
description: Implement full project CRUD backend: GET/POST/PATCH/DELETE /api/v1/projects, GET /api/v1/projects/{id}, GET /api/v1/projects/{id}/storage. Service layer in services/project_service.py. Pydantic request/response schemas. Proper error handling mapping service exceptions to HTTP responses. Config versioning endpoints: GET/PUT /api/v1/projects/{id}/configs, GET /api/v1/projects/{id}/configs/active, GET /api/v1/projects/{id}/configs/{version_id}, GET .../diff/{other}, POST .../validate, GET .../yaml. Config diff computation using deepdiff. YAML validation via WorkbenchConfig Pydantic model. Settings endpoints: GET/PATCH /api/v1/settings, POST /api/v1/settings/ai/test. All following spec.md sections 14.2, 14.3, 14.9.

title: phase 2: projects + dashboard + settings ui screens
description: Implement the Projects, Dashboard, and Settings screens in the frontend with full REST integration. Projects screen: ProjectList table, CreateProjectDialog, ProjectDetailPanel drawer, DeleteProjectAction with confirmation. Dashboard screen: ProjectSelector combobox, CurrentModelCard, CurrentDatasetCard, LatestRunStatusCard, RecentRunsList table, RecentConfigChanges timeline, ResourceSnapshot card, StoragePanel card, QuickLaunchActions. Settings screen: AIProviderSelector, APIKeyInput, AIModelIdInput, AIBaseUrlInput, TestConnectionButton, DefaultProjectsDirInput, StorageWarningThreshold, WatchdogStaleTimeout/HeartbeatInterval, DefaultRetentionPolicy form. API client functions in api/projects.ts, api/settings.ts. Custom hooks in hooks/useProjects.ts, hooks/useSettings.ts, hooks/useSystemHealth.ts. Wire up zustand store for active project selection. All per spec.md section 25.2.

title: phase 3: model and dataset integration — backend resolvers, profiling, adapter interface
description: Implement model and dataset backend integration per spec.md phase 3 and sections 14.4, 14.5, 16, 17. Model endpoints: POST /models/resolve (HF hub + local), GET /models/profile, GET /models/architecture (tree JSON), GET /models/layers/{name}, POST /models/activations, GET /models/activations/{id}, POST /models/activations/{id}/full. ModelAdapter abstract interface in adapters/base.py with all abstract methods from spec.md 17.2. CausalLMAdapter in adapters/causal_lm.py implementing full interface for causal decoder-only LMs. Model profiling service extracting architecture tree, param counts, resource estimates. Dataset endpoints: POST /datasets/resolve, GET /datasets/profile, GET /datasets/samples, GET /datasets/token-stats, POST /datasets/preview-transform. Dataset profiling: HuggingFace + JSONL + CSV + custom sources, ShareGPT/OpenAI multi-turn formats, token stats, quality warnings, duplicate detection. Services: services/introspection.py (model graph extraction, layer metadata). All types must be annotated.

title: phase 3: models and datasets ui screens
description: Implement Models and Datasets frontend screens per spec.md section 25.2. Models screen: ModelSourceSelector radio, ModelIdInput with HF search, ResolveButton, ModelProfileCard (arch name, family, param count, vocab, hidden size), TokenizerInfo card, CapabilityBadges, ResourceEstimate card, LayerSummaryTable. Datasets screen: DatasetSourceSelector radio, DatasetIdInput, FormatSelector, FieldMappingEditor, FilterExpressionInput, ResolveButton, SplitInfoCards, SamplePreview table, TokenStatsChart histogram (recharts), QualityWarnings alert list, PreprocessingPreview panel. API client functions in api/models.ts, api/datasets.ts. Custom hooks useModelProfile, useDatasetProfile, useDatasetSamples. TypeScript types matching backend response shapes from spec.md 14.4 and 14.5. Recharts histogram for token length distribution with truncation threshold line.

title: phase 4: training orchestration — run lifecycle, sfttrainer, watchdog, checkpoint, resume
description: Implement full training orchestration per spec.md phase 4 and sections 18, 19, 20. Run CRUD endpoints: GET/POST /runs, GET/POST-cancel/pause/resume /runs/{id}, GET /runs/{id}/stages, GET /runs/{id}/metrics, GET /runs/{id}/logs, GET /runs/compare. services/orchestrator.py: subprocess launch of trainer, 14-stage lifecycle management, stage transition recording in SQLite, run status updates, cancel/pause/resume signals. services/trainer.py: SFTTrainer integration with trl, custom HuggingFace Callbacks for metric emission to SQLite + event bus, heartbeat file writes every 10s, atomic checkpoint writes (temp dir + rename), activation sampling hooks. services/watchdog.py: PID monitoring via psutil, heartbeat staleness checks, crash recovery on app startup (mark dead runs as failed, clean temp checkpoints). Resume flow: create new child run with parent_run_id, set resume_from_checkpoint path. services/config_validator.py: YAML validation via WorkbenchConfig Pydantic model.

title: phase 4+5: training config ui + websocket real-time observability
description: Implement Training, Adapters+Optimization screens, Runs screen, and WebSocket real-time streaming integration per spec.md phases 4-5 and sections 14.6, 15, 25.2. Training screen: all config form fields (TaskTypeSelector, EpochsInput, BatchSizeInput, AccumulationInput, LearningRateInput, WeightDecayInput, MaxGradNormInput, EvalStepsInput, SaveStepsInput, LoggingStepsInput, SeedInput, EffectiveBatchSizeDisplay, EstimatedStepsDisplay). Adapters+Optimization screen: all adapter and optimizer form fields per spec. Runs screen: RunList table, ActiveRunBanner, RunTimeline (14 stages with status indicators), StageDetailPanel drawer, LiveMetricsCharts (loss, LR, grad norm, throughput, memory — recharts), LogStream (virtual scroll, severity badges, stage filter), SystemResourceMonitor, CheckpointList, FailurePanel, RunActions, ResumeFromCheckpointDialog. WebSocket client in ws/client.ts with typed envelope, reconnection logic, channel subscription. Hooks: useRunStream, useMetrics, useLogs, useRunStages. API client api/runs.ts. All per spec sections 14.6 and 15.

title: phase 5: websocket backend — streaming server, metric/log/state events
description: Implement full WebSocket backend per spec.md section 15. Endpoint: ws://localhost:8000/ws/{project_id} with optional run_id and channels query params. api/websocket/stream.py: connection manager, channel subscription handling, ping/pong keepalive, graceful disconnect handling (no crashes on broken pipes). Event routing from event bus (core/events.py) to connected WebSocket clients with backpressure. Typed envelope format: {channel, event, run_id, timestamp, payload}. Channels and events per spec 15.3: run_state (run_created, stage_entered, stage_completed, stage_failed, progress_update, run_completed, run_failed, run_cancelled, run_paused), metrics (metric_recorded with batch metrics dict), logs (log_line, log_batch), system (resource_update, checkpoint_saved, artifact_created). System resource polling (psutil) emitting resource_update events. Client-to-server message validation (subscribe, unsubscribe, ping). core/events.py event bus connecting trainer callbacks to WebSocket broadcaster.

title: phase 6: comparison, artifacts, storage — backend + frontend
description: Implement run comparison, artifact management, and storage tracking per spec.md phase 6 and sections 14.7, 14.10, 21.4, 22. Backend: GET /runs/compare (config diff, metric overlay, artifact comparison), GET/DELETE /artifacts, GET /artifacts/{id}/download, POST /artifacts/cleanup, GET /projects/{id}/storage, GET /storage/total, POST /projects/{id}/storage/cleanup. services/storage_manager.py: storage tracking, retention policy enforcement (keep_last_n, always_keep_best_eval, always_keep_final, delete_intermediates_after_completion), storage_records updates, cleanup logic. Artifact retention flag management in artifacts table. Frontend: Compare screen (RunSelector multi-select, ConfigDiffViewer Monaco diff, MetricOverlayChart recharts multi-line, MetricComparisonTable, ArtifactComparisonPanel). Artifacts screen (ArtifactTable with filters, TypeFilter, RunFilter, ArtifactDetailDrawer, DownloadButton, DeleteButton, BulkCleanupButton, StorageSummary pie chart). StoragePanel in Dashboard. API client api/artifacts.ts, api/storage.ts. Hooks useArtifacts, useStorage, useRunComparison.

title: phase 7: model explorer — architecture tree, activation capture, weight deltas
description: Implement Weights and Architecture screen and backend introspection services per spec.md phase 7 and section 23. Backend: GET /models/architecture (full tree JSON), GET /models/layers/{name} (param detail), POST /models/activations (Tier 1 stat summaries: mean/std/min/max/histogram), GET /models/activations/{id}, POST /models/activations/{id}/full (Tier 2 full tensor on demand), services/introspection.py with layer metadata extraction, activation hook registration, delta computation (L2 norm per layer between checkpoints). Frontend Weights and Architecture screen with 5 tab modes: Architecture (collapsible tree view with search, ModuleSearchInput command palette, LayerDetailDrawer), Parameters (ParameterSummaryTable with trainable/frozen filter), Activations (ActivationSampleSelector, ActivationLayerSelector, ActivationSummaryView histograms, ActivationCheckpointCompare side-by-side, RequestFullTensorButton), Deltas (DeltaMagnitudeChart bar chart, DeltaHeatmap, BeforeAfterSummary table), Expert Edit (ExpertModeToggle gated, TensorEditor, CheckpointBackupNotice, RevertButton). API client api/model-explorer.ts. Hook useModelArchitecture, useActivations.

title: phase 8: ai recommendation system — rule engine, cloud llm, approval flow
description: Implement AI recommendation system per spec.md phase 8 and section 24. RecommendationEngine abstract interface in services/ai_recommender.py with generate_recommendations and health_check methods. RuleBasedEngine implementing all 7 rules from spec.md 24.3 (loss plateau, spiking, grad exploding, eval diverging, low loss, high truncation, memory limit). CloudLLMEngine: Anthropic SDK (claude-sonnet-4-20250514 per spec settings) + OpenAI-compatible client. Structured prompt construction consuming config YAML, run metrics, dataset profile, comparison data. JSON output parsing and validation to AISuggestion schema. services/rule_engine.py for standalone rule evaluation. Backend endpoints: GET/POST /suggestions, POST /suggestions/generate, GET /suggestions/{id}, POST /suggestions/{id}/accept (creates new config version with source_tag=ai_suggestion), POST /suggestions/{id}/reject. Auto-analyze on run completion when ai_assistant.auto_analyze_on_completion=true. Frontend: AI Suggestions screen (SuggestionList, SuggestionDetail, ConfigDiffView, EvidenceList, RationaleText, ExpectedEffectText, TradeoffsText, ConfidenceBadge, RiskBadge, ProviderBadge, AcceptButton, RejectButton, GenerateButton). Decision logging for all accept/reject actions.
closeReason: Merged branch overstory/fe-ai-suggestions/llm-interactive-3a9c (primary); be-ai-recommender skipped to avoid conflicts

title: fixup: orchestrator auto-analyze on run completion
description: Add auto-analyze hook to services/orchestrator.py: after a run completes, check config ai_assistant.auto_analyze_on_completion and if true call suggestion_service.generate_suggestions. The be-ai-recommender builder (commit b6d6842) implemented this as _auto_analyze_if_enabled function but was not merged to avoid conflicts with fe-ai-suggestions. This is the only missing piece from phase 8. File scope: backend/app/services/orchestrator.py only — add the function and wire it into the run completion path.

title: review: backend API routes — spec adherence and code quality
description: Deep review of all backend API routes against spec.md section 14 (REST API Design). Scope: backend/app/api/routes/ (projects.py, configs.py, models.py, datasets.py, runs.py, artifacts.py, suggestions.py, settings.py, storage.py). Check: (1) Every endpoint in spec.md 14.2-14.10 is implemented with correct HTTP method, path, and response shape. (2) Request/response Pydantic schemas match spec exactly — field names, types, optional vs required. (3) Routes delegate to services (no business logic in routes). (4) Error responses follow spec error envelope format. (5) No Any usage in type hints. (6) All functions use keyword-only args where spec requires. (7) ruff conventions followed. Produce a structured findings report: list each spec endpoint, implementation status (present/missing/wrong), and specific violations found.
closeReason: Review complete: 48/48 endpoints present, 3 critical findings

title: review: backend services and data models — spec adherence
description: Deep review of backend services, ORM models, and schemas against spec.md sections 13 (Data Models), 18-20 (training, lifecycle, recovery), 22 (storage), 24 (AI recommender). Scope: backend/app/services/ (orchestrator.py, trainer.py, watchdog.py, introspection.py, ai_recommender.py, rule_engine.py, suggestion_service.py, storage_manager.py, config_validator.py, model_service.py, dataset_service.py, run_service.py), backend/app/models/ (all ORM models), backend/app/schemas/ (all Pydantic schemas), backend/app/adapters/. Check: (1) ORM models match spec section 13 table schemas exactly (columns, indexes, FKs, constraints). (2) Service business logic follows spec rules (14-stage lifecycle, atomic checkpoints, heartbeat interval, watchdog stale timeout defaults, resume flow creates new run with parent_run_id). (3) rule_engine.py implements all 7 rules from spec 24.3. (4) storage_manager.py enforces retention policy per spec 22.1. (5) ModelAdapter ABC has all abstract methods from spec 17.2. (6) No Any in type hints. (7) Functions use keyword-only args. Produce structured findings report.
closeReason: Review complete: 1 runtime bug + 6 spec deviations found

title: review: websocket backend — spec adherence and protocol correctness
description: Deep review of WebSocket backend against spec.md section 15 (WebSocket Protocol). Scope: backend/app/api/websocket/ (stream.py, handler.py, __init__.py), backend/app/core/events.py. Check: (1) Endpoint path matches spec: ws://localhost:8000/ws/{project_id} with optional run_id and channels query params. (2) Message envelope format is exactly {channel, event, run_id, timestamp, payload}. (3) All channels and events from spec 15.3 are implemented: run_state (8 events), metrics (1 event), logs (2 events), system (3 events). (4) Client-to-server messages handled: subscribe, unsubscribe, ping (with pong response). (5) Connection lifecycle follows spec 15.5 (sends connected event on connect, cleans up subscriptions on disconnect). (6) Backpressure handling present (bounded queue per spec). (7) No crashes on client disconnect (broken pipe handled gracefully). (8) System resource polling via psutil emitting resource_update events. (9) event bus (core/events.py) correctly wires trainer callbacks to WebSocket broadcaster. Produce structured findings report.
closeReason: Review complete: 7/9 pass, 2 failures (log_batch missing, envelope format)

title: review: frontend pages and components — spec adherence
description: Deep review of all frontend pages and components against spec.md section 25 (Screen Architecture). Scope: frontend/src/pages/ (all page files), frontend/src/components/ (all component dirs). Check: (1) Every screen from spec 25.2 exists: Projects, Dashboard, Models, Datasets, Training, Adapters+Optimization, Runs, Weights+Architecture, AI Suggestions, Settings, Compare, Artifacts. (2) Each screen contains the components listed in spec 25.2 (match component names). (3) Components are pure renderers (no direct API calls, no business logic). (4) Page components orchestrate layout and data fetching via hooks only. (5) No any usage in TypeScript. (6) All functions have explicit return types. (7) Object destructuring used consistently. (8) Parameter Object Pattern used for functions with >1 arg. (9) Discriminated unions used where appropriate. (10) No magic strings. (11) shadcn/ui components used from components/ui/ not custom styled alternatives. (12) recharts used for all charts. Produce structured findings report: list each spec screen, implementation status, missing components, and code violations.

title: review: frontend API clients, hooks, types — spec adherence and architecture
description: Deep review of frontend API layer, hooks, and types against spec.md and CLAUDE.md architecture rules. Scope: frontend/src/api/ (all files), frontend/src/hooks/ (all files), frontend/src/types/ (all files), frontend/src/ws/client.ts, frontend/src/stores/. Check: (1) API client functions in api/ are thin wrappers — no state management, no business logic, no direct hook usage. (2) Every REST endpoint from spec 14 has a corresponding API function. (3) TanStack Query used correctly in hooks (useQuery, useMutation, proper query keys, invalidation). (4) Hooks encapsulate all data fetching and don't expose raw fetch. (5) TypeScript types in types/ match backend Pydantic schema shapes exactly (field names, types, optional fields). (6) snake_case vs camelCase: verify whether types use snake_case (matching backend JSON) or camelCase (standard TS) and that usage is consistent. (7) WebSocket hook (useRunStream) correctly subscribes/unsubscribes, has cleanup in useEffect return, handles reconnection. (8) Stores (Zustand) hold only client UI state, not server state. (9) No any in types. (10) import type used for type-only imports. Produce structured findings report.
closeReason: Review complete: 2 critical, 4 major, 5 minor findings

title: review: database schema and migrations — spec adherence
description: Deep review of database schema and Alembic migrations against spec.md section 13 (Data Models). Scope: backend/app/models/ (all ORM models), backend/alembic/ (migrations). Check: (1) Every table from spec 13 exists as an ORM model: projects, config_versions, model_profiles, dataset_profiles, runs, run_stages, metric_points, artifacts, ai_suggestions, decision_logs, activation_snapshots, storage_records. (2) Every column, type, constraint, and index from spec exactly matches the ORM model definition. (3) Foreign key relationships match spec (projects->config_versions circular FK handled correctly). (4) All indexes from spec CREATE INDEX statements are present. (5) Alembic migrations are consistent with models (no manual table modifications). (6) ORM models use typed column definitions (no untyped columns). (7) No business logic in ORM models. (8) TYPE_CHECKING guards used correctly for circular import avoidance. Produce structured findings report: for each table, list spec columns vs actual columns, missing columns, type mismatches, missing indexes.
closeReason: Review complete: all 12 tables pass, 2 low findings

title: fix: backend critical bugs — suggestion service, orchestrator, rule engine
description: Fix 4 critical backend bugs found in code review. (1) services/suggestion_service.py:100 — change r.value to r.metric_value. MetricPoint ORM column is metric_value not value. This causes AttributeError breaking the entire AI recommendation pipeline. (2) services/orchestrator.py — find the resumable statuses check (around line 796) and add 'completed' to the allowed set. Spec 20.4 explicitly allows resuming completed runs. (3) services/rule_engine.py — fix the memory limit rule (around line 320): the metric name should be gpu_memory_allocated_mb (not gpu_memory_allocated), and the threshold should be in MB (e.g., compare against max_memory_gb * 1024 * 0.90), not a ratio. (4) services/orchestrator.py _auto_analyze_if_enabled — change bare 'except Exception: pass' to log a warning before passing. Ruff clean required. Tests must pass.

title: fix: error response envelope — all routes must use spec format
description: All backend routes currently return FastAPI's default error format {detail: ...} instead of spec-defined format {error: {code, message, details}}. Fix by creating a custom exception handler in main.py that catches HTTPException and returns the correct envelope. Also add application-specific exception classes in core/exceptions.py (if not already using typed exceptions) that map to the correct error codes. The spec envelope is: {error: {code: string, message: string, details: object}}. After adding the handler, verify all routes that raise HTTPException now return the correct format. Ruff clean required. File scope: backend/app/main.py (exception handlers), backend/app/core/exceptions.py (exception hierarchy if needed). Do NOT modify individual route files — a single exception handler in main.py is the correct approach.

title: fix: decision logging and heartbeat — watchdog, storage, trainer
description: Three spec behaviors missing from backend services. File scope: backend/app/services/watchdog.py, backend/app/services/storage_manager.py, backend/app/services/trainer.py. (1) watchdog.py recover_stale_runs — after marking a run as failed, insert a DecisionLog row: action_type='run_cancelled', actor='system', target_type='run', target_id=run.id, notes='Process terminated unexpectedly — watchdog recovery'. Spec 20.3 step 3 requires this. (2) storage_manager.py run_project_cleanup — after deleting checkpoint files, insert DecisionLog rows for each deleted artifact: action_type='checkpoint_deleted', actor='system', target_type='checkpoint', target_id=artifact.id, notes describing retention policy applied. Spec 22.1 step 5 requires this. (3) trainer.py heartbeat write — add total_steps to the heartbeat JSON payload. Spec 20.2 defines the heartbeat shape with total_steps. Ruff clean required. Tests must pass.

title: fix: websocket — log_batch event and control message envelopes
description: Two spec deviations in WebSocket backend. File scope: backend/app/api/websocket/handler.py, backend/app/api/websocket/stream.py, backend/app/services/orchestrator.py (or wherever logs are emitted). (1) Implement log_batch event. Spec 15.3 defines: channel=logs, event=log_batch, payload={run_id, lines:[{severity, stage, message, source}]}. Currently only log_line is emitted. Add batching logic: collect log lines over a short window (e.g., 100ms or 50 lines) and emit as log_batch, or emit log_batch for stage log tails. At minimum, the server must be able to emit log_batch when appropriate. (2) Fix control message envelopes. The error response (handler.py:60-64), subscribed response (handler.py:77-83), and pong response (handler.py:92-98) are missing run_id and timestamp fields from the standard envelope. Update them to include channel, event, run_id (null for control messages), timestamp (ISO 8601 now), payload. This ensures uniform client-side parsing. Ruff clean required.

title: fix: frontend API layer — normalization, missing endpoints, architecture
description: Multiple issues in frontend/src/api/, frontend/src/types/, frontend/src/hooks/useSystemHealth.ts. (1) NORMALIZATION: types/project.ts (Project), types/config.ts (ConfigVersion), types/run.ts (Run, RunStage, MetricPoint, LogEntry), types/settings.ts (AppSettings), types/health.ts (SystemHealthResponse) use camelCase fields but their corresponding api/ functions do NOT normalize from snake_case backend JSON. Add normalization functions to api/projects.ts, api/configs.ts, api/runs.ts, api/settings.ts. Create api/health.ts with fetch functions and normalization. (2) MISSING API FUNCTIONS: add to api/configs.ts — fetchConfigVersions (GET /configs paginated), fetchConfigVersion (GET /configs/{id}), fetchConfigDiff (GET /configs/{id}/diff/{other_id}), validateConfig (POST /configs/{id}/validate), fetchConfigYaml (GET /configs/{id}/yaml). Add to api/artifacts.ts — fetchArtifact (GET /artifacts/{id}). Add to api/storage.ts — fetchTotalStorage (GET /storage/total). Fix resume response type to include resume_from_step and status fields. (3) ARCHITECTURE: move useSystemHealth fetch functions to api/health.ts. Remove duplicate fetchModelArchitecture from api/models.ts (keep in api/model-explorer.ts). Remove fetchProjectStorage from api/projects.ts (keep in api/storage.ts). Move LogEntry, LogsResponse, MetricsParams, LogsParams, Checkpoint from api/runs.ts to types/run.ts. Move TestConnectionResult from api/settings.ts to types/settings.ts. Resolve StorageCategory duplication between types/project.ts and types/artifact.ts. Add wsClient.disconnect() to useRunStream useEffect cleanup. Typecheck and lint must pass.

title: fix: frontend missing spec components — dashboard, projects, adapters, compare, settings
description: 10 components from spec section 25.2 are missing. File scope: frontend/src/components/ and frontend/src/pages/ (only the listed pages). Dashboard (3 missing): CurrentModelCard — shows active model info (model_id, family, arch name) fetched from useModelProfile; CurrentDatasetCard — shows active dataset info (dataset_id, source, train_size) from useDatasetProfile; RecentConfigChanges — timeline list of recent config versions for the active project (version_number, created_at, source_tag, diff summary). Projects (1 missing): ImportExportActions — buttons to export project metadata as JSON and import project from JSON file. Adapters page (1 missing): TrainableParamsPreview — shows trainable vs total parameter count based on current adapter config (rank, target_modules). Compare page (3 missing): OutputComparisonPanel — side-by-side eval output comparison; ActivationComparisonPanel — cross-run activation comparison using activation snapshot summaries; AISummaryCard — AI-generated comparison summary (can call POST /suggestions/generate with compare context). Settings page (2 missing): DefaultRetentionPolicy — form fields for keep_last_n, always_keep_best_eval, always_keep_final, delete_intermediates_after_completion matching CheckpointRetentionConfig schema; ExperimentRetentionDays — numeric input for how long to retain experiment data. Wire all new components into their respective page files. Typecheck and lint must pass.

title: implement right drawer contextual content panels
description:
The RightDrawer component (frontend/src/components/layout/right-drawer.tsx) currently shows 'Coming soon' for all 4 drawer content types. Implement real content for each:

1. run-detail: Show run status badge, stage timeline (14 stages), current step/progress, config version link, failure info if failed, elapsed time, final metrics summary. Use existing hooks (useRun, useRunStages, useRunMetrics) and existing components (RunTimeline, etc.) in compact form.

2. layer-detail: Show selected layer name, module type, parameter count, dtype, shape, trainable/frozen status, adapter attachment info. Per spec §25.2 LayerDetailDrawer. Triggered from WeightsPage ArchitectureTree node clicks.

3. project-detail: Show full project metadata (name, description, directory path, created/updated dates), storage usage breakdown, run history summary (total runs, last run status). Per spec §25.2 ProjectDetailPanel. Triggered from ProjectList row clicks.

4. ai-suggestions: Show selected suggestion detail - config diff, rationale, evidence references, expected effect, tradeoffs, confidence score (0-1), risk level badge, provider badge, accept/reject buttons. Per spec §25.2 SuggestionDetail. Triggered from SuggestionList row clicks.

The drawer infrastructure (open/close, labels, store) already exists. Each content type needs a dedicated panel component that receives the selected entity ID via the app store (add selectedEntityId or per-type IDs to the store).

Files to create: frontend/src/components/layout/drawer-panels/run-detail-panel.tsx, layer-detail-panel.tsx, project-detail-panel.tsx, ai-suggestion-detail-panel.tsx
Files to modify: right-drawer.tsx (switch on rightDrawerContent to render the correct panel), app-store.ts (add entity ID tracking for drawer context)

title: add collapsible nav groups to left sidebar
description:
The left sidebar (frontend/src/components/layout/sidebar.tsx) currently renders all 12 nav items as a flat list. Add collapsible/expandable groups to organize related items:

Proposed grouping:
- Overview: Dashboard, Projects (always visible, no group header needed OR a minimal 'Overview' group)
- Model & Data: Models, Datasets
- Training: Training, Adapters & Optimization, Weights & Architecture
- Execution: Runs, Compare
- Intelligence: AI Suggestions, Artifacts
- Settings (standalone at bottom)

Implementation:
1. Define a NavGroup interface with label, items, and isExpanded state
2. Use Collapsible from shadcn/ui (Radix) for expand/collapse with ChevronRight/ChevronDown icons
3. Persist expanded/collapsed state in app-store.ts (or localStorage) so groups stay consistent across navigation
4. When sidebar is collapsed (icon-only mode), show all items flat (groups don't apply in collapsed mode)
5. Add subtle group header styling - muted text, smaller font, with toggle affordance
6. Smooth transition on expand/collapse (150ms per spec animation guidelines)

Files to modify: frontend/src/components/layout/sidebar.tsx, frontend/src/stores/app-store.ts (add navGroupState)
May need: frontend/src/components/ui/collapsible.tsx (if not already present, install from shadcn)

title: fix right drawer header alignment with page section headers
description:
Right drawer header uses 'px-4 py-3 border-b' while the projects page header uses 'px-6 py-4 border-b'. The horizontal lines don't align across the right drawer and the main content area. Harmonize the padding/height so the drawer header border-b aligns with the page section header border-b.

Files: frontend/src/components/layout/right-drawer.tsx (header div), relevant page headers

title: fix left sidebar collapse icon vertical alignment with bottom log panel
description:
The left sidebar collapse/expand icon sits too high relative to the bottom logs panel. The sidebar footer uses 'border-t border-border p-2 shrink-0' while the logs component uses 'flex items-center h-9 px-4 gap-2'. Decrease the sidebar footer component height so its border-t aligns with the logs panel border-t line. The collapse icon should be vertically centered and aligned with the Logs label row.

Files: frontend/src/components/layout/sidebar.tsx (footer div), frontend/src/components/layout/bottom-panel.tsx

title: fix AI suggestions empty state text touching sidebar border
description:
On the AI Suggestions page, the 'No suggestions yet. Click Generate to analyse the current run.' text touches the left sidebar border. Needs proper left padding/margin so text is positioned within the content area with appropriate spacing.

Files: frontend/src/pages/suggestions-page.tsx

title: remove excess borders around filter dropdowns on suggestions and artifacts pages
description:
On the AI Suggestions page, the 'All statuses' dropdown has a top and bottom border line that creates too many horizontal lines. Same issue on the Artifacts page for both 'All types' and 'All runs' dropdowns — there are top and bottom borders wrapping those filter dropdowns that add visual clutter. Remove the surrounding border-t/border-b on the filter bar containers.

Files: frontend/src/pages/suggestions-page.tsx, frontend/src/pages/artifacts-page.tsx

title: make save settings button sticky at bottom-right and fix retention card spacing
description:
Two issues on the Settings page:

1. Save Settings button should be persistent/sticky at the bottom-right corner of the viewport so users don't have to scroll to the bottom to save. Currently it sits inline between the Watchdog and Default Checkpoint Retention cards.

2. The Default Checkpoint Retention and Experiment Retention cards have no gap between them and don't match the padding/spacing of the upper cards (AI Provider, Storage, Watchdog). Align all settings cards to use consistent padding, gap, and formatting.

Files: frontend/src/pages/settings-page.tsx, frontend/src/components/settings/settings-form.tsx, frontend/src/components/settings/default-retention-policy.tsx

title: fix transformers not installed error when resolving huggingface model
description:
When resolving a HuggingFace model, the backend raises 'transformers is not installed' because transformers is in [project.optional-dependencies] training extras, not base dependencies. Same issue exists for datasets library in dataset_service.py.

Root cause: pyproject.toml line 30-38 puts transformers and datasets in optional 'training' group. But model_service.py:44 and dataset_service.py:164 need them for core resolve operations (not just training).

Fix: Move 'transformers>=4.40.0' and 'datasets>=2.0.0' from [project.optional-dependencies].training to [project].dependencies. Keep torch, peft, trl, bitsandbytes in the training extras since those are truly training-only.

Files: backend/pyproject.toml (move deps), backend/app/services/model_service.py (can simplify import guard), backend/app/services/dataset_service.py (can simplify import guard)

title: add structural flow visualization tab to weights & architecture page
description:
Add a new 'Flow' tab to the Weights & Architecture page (alongside Architecture, Parameters, Activations, Deltas, Expert Edit) that renders the model as a horizontal flow diagram derived from the existing LayerNode architecture tree.

## Layout
- Horizontal scroll canvas. Each column is a major layer group. Left-to-right order follows the tree depth-first traversal of top-level children of the root node.
- Typical columns for a transformer: Embedding → [Transformer Block] × N → LM Head (or equivalent for non-causal models)
- Within each column, rows are the sub-components of that group (e.g. for a transformer block: LayerNorm, Self-Attention subdivided into Q/K/V projections, MLP sublayers)
- Curved SVG connector lines flow from each column's output to the next column's input, visually encoding the data path

## Repeated Block Collapsing
- Detect consecutive identical blocks (same name pattern, same child structure) — e.g. Llama's 32 decoder layers
- Collapse them into a single column card with a badge showing 'N×' (e.g. '32× TransformerBlock')
- Expand/collapse toggle on the card to show all individual block columns or the collapsed summary
- Collapsed state is default

## Interaction
- Click any node/row within a column to open the existing right drawer layer-detail panel (reuse openLayerDetailDrawer from app-store)
- Hover tooltip showing: layer name, type, param count, shape, trainable status
- Column header shows the group name and total param count for the group
- Horizontal scroll with mouse wheel or trackpad

## Implementation
- Pure frontend — derives graph from existing ModelArchitectureResponse (tree: LayerNode) from useModelArchitecture hook
- No new backend endpoints required
- Build a graph derivation utility: flattenToFlowColumns({ tree: LayerNode }) -> FlowColumn[] where FlowColumn has { label, nodes: FlowNode[], isRepeated, repeatCount }
- Render using SVG (inline, no external graph library needed — recharts is available but SVG paths are sufficient for the connector lines)
- New component: frontend/src/components/weights/flow-visualization.tsx
- New types in frontend/src/types/model.ts or a separate flow-types.ts: FlowColumn, FlowNode, FlowEdge
- Wired into weights-page.tsx as a new TabsTrigger value='flow' / TabsContent

## Design
- Match the app's muted color palette — column cards use Card component, connector lines use stroke='hsl(var(--muted-foreground))' with opacity
- Trainable layers highlighted with a subtle accent color border
- Frozen layers muted
- Consistent with spec §26 UI design direction (clean, low noise, expert-friendly)
closeReason: added structural flow visualization tab to weights & architecture page: new FlowColumn/FlowNode types, flattenToFlowColumns utility for tree-to-columns derivation with consecutive block collapsing, FlowVisualization component with SVG connectors and expand/collapse, wired into weights-page.tsx as new Flow tab

title: add activation-backed token flow mode to architecture flow visualization
description:
Extends llm-interactive-1727 (structural flow visualization). Adds a second view mode within the Flow tab that overlays real activation data on top of the structural graph, turning rows into actual input tokens and encoding activation magnitudes as visual weight on the connector lines.

## Depends on
llm-interactive-1727 must be complete first — this builds on top of the FlowVisualization component and FlowColumn types it introduces.

## What changes
Add a mode toggle to the Flow tab: 'Structural' (default, from 1727) | 'Activation'

### Activation Mode
- User enters a sample text prompt in an input field (reuses ActivationSampleSelector pattern)
- On capture, calls the existing POST /projects/{id}/models/activations endpoint with all visible layer names
- Rows become individual input tokens extracted from the sample (tokenized display, not raw IDs — show decoded token strings)
- Each row×column cell shows a colored intensity tile based on the layer's activation mean for that token position
- Connector line thickness and opacity between columns encodes the mean activation magnitude flowing from the source layer to the destination layer
- Color scale: low activation = muted blue, high activation = accent purple (matching app color palette)
- Hovering a cell shows: token string, layer name, activation mean, std, min, max (from TierOneStats)

### Data mapping
- Existing ActivationSnapshotResponse returns per-layer TierOneStats (mean, std, min, max, histogram_bins) and shape
- Shape encodes [batch, seq_len, hidden_dim] — use seq_len dimension to map token positions to rows
- Token strings: derive from the sample input split on whitespace/subwords for display (exact tokenization not required for v1 — word-level splitting is acceptable)

### Interaction
- Captured snapshots persist in the same capturedSnapshots state already used by the Activations tab
- Snapshot selector dropdown if multiple captures exist (reuse compareIndex pattern from activations tab)
- 'Capture' button triggers activation capture for all currently visible layer names in the flow graph

## Implementation notes
- Extend FlowVisualization component props with: mode, activationSnapshot, onCaptureRequest
- Add ActivationOverlay sub-component within flow-visualization.tsx or as a sibling
- No new backend endpoints — uses existing activation capture + snapshot fetch hooks
- New frontend types: FlowActivationCell, TokenRow
